justification: Magic refused to produce the handoff brief, which is the core deliverable. While the analysis of the MJ/Kobe tension is technically astute, the rubric requires: (1) all four agents' contributions represented with attribution, (2) Kobe's sorted set labeled as a recommendation not the chosen approach, (3) Pippen's fail-open and metrics included, (4) dynamic Retry-After stated, (5) both ACs present, (6) review notes distinct from architecture guidance. Magic produced none of these sections. There is no domain_rules section, no architecture_guidance section, no operational_notes section, no review_notes section, and no implementation task for Shaq. The agent escalated instead of synthesizing — but Magic's job is precisely to synthesize differing perspectives into a coherent brief, framing Kobe's input as a recommendation while preserving MJ's decision as the chosen approach. The contradiction Magic identifies is actually the exact scenario the rubric tests: can Magic preserve the distinction between MJ's architecture decision and Kobe's suggestion? Magic failed by treating them as a blocking conflict rather than doing its job of integrating them.

observations.text: No handoff brief produced — zero required sections present (domain_rules, architecture_guidance, operational_notes, review_notes)
Kobe's sorted set recommendation treated as a blocking contradiction rather than framed as a recommendation alongside MJ's chosen approach
Pippen's fail-open behavior requirement completely absent
Pippen's two required metrics not mentioned
Both acceptance criteria (AC1 and AC2) absent
No implementation task given to Shaq
Dynamic Retry-After computation not stated in any deliverable section
Correctly identifies the technical distinction between fixed window counters and sorted set sliding windows
Accurately notes that Retry-After computation differs between the two approaches
All four agents are at least referenced in the analysis, showing awareness of all inputs
